name: 2nd Avenue Theatre

evidence: History is difficult to find about this theatre; into the 1960's, until demolition for new buildings

name: AMC Empire 25

name: American Theatre
evidence: built in 1914; still operating as a movie theatre in December 1969; demolished on March 28, 2019

name: Annex Theatre
evidence: Closed and Demolished status indicated, but no specific years provided

name: Arena Theatre
evidence: it opened in 1918; operated until 1948-49, when it was demolished to make way for the Port Authority Bus Terminal

name: Atlas Theatre
evidence: operated at this site from at least 1915 (possibly as early as 1912) until 1923 or later; gone by 1929

name: Belasco Theatre
evidence: The Stuyvesant Theatre was opened on October 16, 1907

evidence: opened prior to 1926; had closed by 1931

evidence: It was an airdome which had opened by 1914.

name: Cine 42

name: Cinema Luna
evidence: ending its days as an adult movie theatre named Jade Cinema and finally named Cinema Luna; no specific years

name: Circus Cinema
evidence: The Circus Cinema opened on March 2, 1971; The Circus Cinema was closed in 1996.

name: Clinton Theatre

name: Columbia Cinema
evidence: Built in 1913; It closed in 1993 and became a Lucille Roberts gym. It has now been demolished.

name: Criterion Theatre
evidence: opened September 16, 1936; finally closed on May 4, 2000 and was gutted to become a Toys R Us store

name: David Cinema
evidence: opened as the New David Cinema around 1978. It was closed in June 1995.

name: Doll Theater
evidence: another one of Times Squares' XXX theatres that flourished in the 1970's and 1980's

name: East 86th Street Cinemas
evidence: Built in 1904, by 1914 it was operating as a 1,000-seat movie theatre; closed it on May 10, 2019

name: Elysee Theatre

name: Eros Theatre
evidence: The Eros 1 Theatre was opened in 1969; The Eros Theatre closed in 1997.

name: Film Forum 1 & 2
evidence: operating from September 9, 1981 until September 4, 1989 when closed and was demolished

name: Fort Jay Theatre

evidence: opened in around 1917; the Greenwich Theater was operating in 1927

evidence: Oscar Hammerstein I opened the Harlem Opera House in 1889, his first New York theater.
